yesterdayAnalysis: Friday's 28-mile easy bike ride (TSS 43, HR 121) was a perfect execution of the scheduled shake-out ride — moderate effort with no intensity, keeping legs fresh for today's 10K race. The controlled heart rate and TSS show appropriate restraint 15 days out from Oregon 70.3.
todayRecommendation: Execute the 4th of July 10K at strong tempo effort, targeting 8:00-8:15/mi pace. This is NOT an all-out race — treat it as a controlled race-pace stimulus with Oregon 15 days away. Be especially cautious on downhills to protect the right hip, and prioritize smooth pacing over podium chasing.
weekProgress: Week 11 of 13 shows 282 TSS of 340 target (83% complete) with 1 day remaining. Given the back-loaded schedule and today's 10K race still to come, the week is tracking well numerically. However, Thursday's scheduled 3x8min race-effort bike session appears to have been replaced with an easier run, missing the final race-pace bike stimulus before Oregon taper.
keyInsight: Last night's sleep was severely compromised (41 score, 5.1h duration, only 17min REM) heading into today's 10K race — prioritize conservative pacing and listen carefully to body signals during the race.
